text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real yet-another-react-lightbox repository at the commit where the feature gap below needs closing. Implement the feature in the library source.

Context: this library renders a photo/video lightbox carousel. It ships a Zoom plugin that adds pinch/scroll/double-click zoom interactions to the currently displayed slide. Right now the Zoom plugin only ever recognizes image slides -- internally it decides whether to wrap a slide in its interactive zoom container purely by checking whether that slide is an image slide, with no way to opt any other kind of slide into zoom. Consumers can render arbitrary custom slide types through the lightbox's `render.slide` render-prop (keyed by a slide's `type` field, e.g. a custom map view, a video player, a PDF page, etc.), but none of those custom slide types can ever get zoom behavior, no matter what the consumer configures -- the plugin just renders them completely unwrapped, with no zoom UI, no pinch/scroll/double-click handling, nothing.

Feature request: add a way for a consumer to opt specific custom slide types into the same zoom wrapper/gesture handling that image slides already get. Concretely, the Zoom plugin's options object (the `zoom` prop passed to the lightbox) should grow two new optional properties:

- `supports`: a list of slide `type` strings. When the currently displayed slide's `type` is included in this list, the plugin should wrap it in the same interactive zoom container an image slide gets (so it participates in zoom in/out, pinch, scroll-to-zoom, double-click/double-tap, etc., exactly like an image slide does) -- even though the slide itself isn't an image slide. A slide whose `type` is not image and not listed in `supports` (or when `supports` is not configured at all) should continue to render completely unwrapped, exactly as today.

- `maxZoom`: controls how far a non-image slide can be zoomed in. For image slides the maximum zoom level is already computed automatically from the image's natural resolution vs. its displayed size, but that computation is meaningless for a non-image custom slide (there's no natural image resolution to compare against), so it needs its own configurable ceiling. `maxZoom` may be a plain number, or a function that receives the current slide and returns either a number or `undefined`; returning `undefined` (or omitting `maxZoom` entirely) should fall back to a sensible default maximum zoom level of 8x. This setting only affects non-image slides -- image slides keep using their existing resolution-based maximum zoom calculation unchanged.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (Node, npm). You can scope the existing Vitest suite to whatever file you're iterating on with `npx vitest run <path-to-file>`. Implement the feature in the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

message: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
`--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.

text: I’ll trace the Zoom plugin’s slide-type gating and maximum-zoom calculation, then update the public option types, runtime behavior, and focused tests. I’ll preserve the existing image path and verify custom supported slides plus the unchanged unsupported-slide behavior.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "pwd && rg --files | sed -n '1,120p' && rg -n \"supports|maxZoom|Zoom|isImage|render\\.slide|zoom\" packages src test tests 2>/dev/null | head -240"

/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/plugins/zoom/Zoom.tsx 2>/dev/null || true; sed -n '1,220p' src/plugins/zoom/ZoomWrapper.tsx; sed -n '1,140p' src/plugins/zoom/hooks/useZoomImageRect.ts; sed -n '1,150p' src/plugins/zoom/hooks/useZoomState.ts"

/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,180p' src/plugins/zoom/index.ts; sed -n '1,120p' src/plugins/zoom/props.ts; rg -n \"ZoomWrapper|useZoomImageRect|useZoomState|render.*slide|slide.type|isImageSlide\" src test/unit/plugins/Zoom.spec.ts"

text: The current implementation has two separate image assumptions: `Zoom.tsx` decides whether to mount the wrapper, while the controller marks zoom disabled unless the current slide has an image source, and the rectangle hook derives the ceiling only from image dimensions. I’m checking the module/context lifecycle now so supported custom slides receive a valid wrapper ref and bounds without disturbing image resolution handling.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"type Slide|interface Slide|Slide.*type|SlideVideo|SlideImage\" src/types.ts src | head -120; sed -n '1,120p' src/plugins/video/Video.tsx"

text: The clean integration point is to make “zoom-supported” a shared predicate: images remain supported automatically, and non-images are supported only when their `type` appears in `zoom.supports`. The custom path will use `slideRect` as its base content bounds and the configured ceiling (default `8`), which enables scaling while retaining the existing image natural-resolution calculation unchanged. I’m adding this to the plugin options and controller hooks, then I’ll exercise the public behavior through the existing test harness.

text: The first compile pass exposed only a repository-wide TypeScript 6 deprecation setting before reaching the changed code. I also found two typing details to tighten: custom slides do not have an image `src`, and the custom maximum resolver should always produce a numeric ceiling. I’m correcting those and adding focused assertions for wrapper mounting, default/function ceilings, and opt-out rendering.

text: The focused suite now passes, including the default `8x` fallback and function-valued ceiling. The DOM confirms supported custom content is wrapped in the same `.yarl__slide_wrapper`, while unsupported content remains direct. I’m doing a production TypeScript/Rollup build and lint pass next; this should catch declaration-level issues that Vitest transpilation does not type-check.

text: The full unit suite passes: 13 files and 78 tests. The separate type-example command fails on pre-existing export/declaration issues in the repository’s test app (`CaptionsRef`, `ZoomRef`, etc.), unrelated to this change; the production JS build and targeted lint both pass. I’m reviewing the final diff for accidental generated files and one last API consistency check.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc 'git status --short; git diff --check; npx eslint src/plugins/zoom test/unit/plugins/Zoom.spec.ts'

text: The final review found the Zoom documentation still says custom slide types are unsupported, which would contradict the new public API. I’m updating that small documentation section and the option table so the shipped behavior and documented contract stay aligned.

text:
Implemented custom-slide zoom support.

Changes include:

- Added `zoom.supports` and `zoom.maxZoom`.
- Added default custom-slide maximum zoom of `8x`.
- Supports function-valued `maxZoom`.
- Preserved image resolution-based max zoom behavior.
- Added focused tests and updated Zoom documentation.

Verification: production build, lint, and all 78 unit tests pass. The type-example suite still fails on unrelated pre-existing export errors.
